cit.basis: Flat 15% CIT under the Income Tax Act 1995, administered by the Mauritius Revenue Authority (MRA).

gbc_partial_exemption.note: Global Business / partial exemption regime: 80% exemption on qualifying foreign-source income (foreign dividends, interest, ship and aircraft leasing, CIS and closed-end fund management, etc.), leaving 15% on the 20% taxable slice — an effective 3% rate, subject to substance requirements.

corporate_climate_responsibility_levy.note: 2% Corporate Climate Responsibility (CCR) levy on chargeable income of companies with turnover above Rs 50m, introduced by the Finance Act 2024 (2024-25).

vat.note: VAT at 15% (Value Added Tax Act 1998); registration threshold and zero-rated/exempt supplies apply.

personal_income_tax.note: Progressive PAYE with a top rate of 20% under the reformed bracket system; out of scope for this CIT estimator.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; the Mauritian fiscal year runs 1 July to 30 June, so transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Partial exemption applies only to qualifying income streams and requires substance; blended income books alter outcomes.
CCR levy, export rate, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official MRA guidance and a local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
